comment_text.sn35.154:1.2: In SN 48.19, “accomplished in the faculties” is defined as someone who has mastered the five spiritual faculties.

root_text.sn35.154:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.154:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, indriyasampanno hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.154:2.1: “Cakkhundriye ce, bhikkhu, udayabbayānupassī viharanto cakkhundriye nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.154:2.2: jivhindriye ce, bhikkhu, udayabbayānupassī viharanto jivhindriye nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.154:2.3: manindriye ce, bhikkhu, udayabbayānupassī viharanto manindriye nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.154:2.4: Nibbindaṁ virajjati …pe… vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.154:2.5: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.

translation_text.sn35.154:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn35.154:1.2: “Sir, they speak of someone who is ‘accomplished in the faculties’.
translation_text.sn35.154:1.3: How is someone accomplished in the faculties defined?”
translation_text.sn35.154:2.1: “Mendicant, if someone meditates observing rise and fall in the eye faculty, they grow disillusioned with the eye faculty.
translation_text.sn35.154:2.2: If they meditate observing rise and fall in the ear faculty … nose faculty … tongue faculty … body faculty …
translation_text.sn35.154:2.3: mind faculty, they grow disillusioned with the mind faculty.
translation_text.sn35.154:2.4: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. … When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.154:2.5: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.154:2.6: This is how someone who is accomplished in the faculties is defined.”
